plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-266 RP07.P08.M04.S13-RP07.P08.M06.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-266 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP07.P08.M04 secondary workflow tail (PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK semantics through operator summary), the full M05 permission/audit Hermes binding cycle (22 rows), and the M06 synthetic fixture head (8 rows) across three sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-267 handoff; the shared Hermes receipt guards are validated across every section, without opening command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, AI runtime dispatch, search/OCR/index/embedding runtime, state writes, object storage, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-266 is a descriptor-only RP07 search-core closeout slice (risk B, 40 units, RP07.P08.M04.S13-RP07.P08.M06.S08) and is internally consistent and well-substantiated. The pack identity and distributions agree across the registry binding (packages/search/src/registry.js:559), requirements (registry.js:2798), the contract projection (contracts/search-core-contract.json:16, :34560, :35818), the manifest, the README (packages/search/README.md:129), and the live closeout-pack-plan.json:412 — risk_class B, unit_count 40, deliverable distribution {implementation:18, hermes_evidence:19, test:2, claude_review:1}, and micro-title distribution {Secondary Workflow Slice:10, Permission And Audit Binding:22, Synthetic Fixture Set:8}. Continuity holds: CP00-265 (last unit RP07.P08.M04.S12, next_pack CP00-266) feeds CP00-266 (first unit RP07.P08.M04.S13, next_pack CP00-267). All seven required public exports exist (service.js:4222/4255; validators.js:6531/6566/6651/6672/6695), are surfaced via the index.js wildcard re-export, and are imported by both the validator script and model.test.js. The implementation is genuinely side-effect-free: a grep across packages/search/src for writeFile/fetch/exec/child_process/db/network/process.env returned no matches, and the no_write_attestation enumerates every runtime/write surface as false (search/OCR/index/embedding dispatch, command runtime, audit writes, product-state writes, object storage, real fixture data, raw payloads). The descriptor no-leak guards are non-vacuous — the row-key guard objects (permission_summary_receipt/audit_summary_receipt/blocked_claim_receipt/no_real_data_receipt/claude_dependency_marker/human_approval_marker/hermes_command_matrix/validation_command_check at service.js:3948-3962) supply the exact flags the CP266 validator asserts, so validateSearchCoreCp266HermesBindingSliceDescriptor materially enforces descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe/no-raw-payload plus permission/audit/blocked-claim/real-data exclusions. The authority boundary is preserved throughout: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, claude_packet.read_only=true (service.js:4267-4285; contract authority_boundary at :35834), and Hermes is used only as deterministic packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime. There is no production-readiness overclaim: command-evidence.json and the manifest both set production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review / implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout, and production_ready_candidate is correctly derived from coverage.valid && slice.valid rather than asserting enterprise trust. CP178+ review hardening is intact: forbidden_review_evidence (registry.js:742) rejects auth failures, zero-byte output, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing required fields, and the readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON. No P0/P1/P2 issues were found. One cosmetic P3 was noted in the review-harness scope metadata (a path dual-classified as both an active-untracked implementation file and an unrelated preserved file); it does not affect deliverables, validation, or closeout. The unrelated untracked LDIP file (docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) and the v2.0 .docx are correctly treated as out of scope. Note: this reviewer is a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approval authority; enterprise trust requires human final approval, which remains deferred.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-classifies the pack's own manifest directory as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: The path docs/closeout-packs/cp00-266/ (the active pack's own manifest + command-evidence directory) appears in untracked_implementation_files (line 97) and simultaneously in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 103); docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is similarly dual-listed (lines 96 and 102). This is a cosmetic inconsistency in the review-harness scope-metadata artifact, not in the pack deliverables. active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (line 99), so no in-scope file was wrongly excluded from review, and the pack's manifest/command-evidence were inspected directly. No deliverable, validation command, or closeout gate is affected.
findings.recommendation: Have the changed-file-scope generator exclude the active pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack-id>/ directory (and recognized process handoff docs) from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved when they are already listed as untracked implementation files, to remove the ambiguous dual classification.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Cosmetic metadata quirk in the review harness artifact; does not affect CP00-266 implementation, tests, validation evidence, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, or closeout. Recorded for maintainer cleanup only.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-266 search core hermes binding slice descriptor pack
